project.name: TUGAS-AKHIR-VAR-TATA-SURYA
project.currentFocus: Validate larger mobile AR solar scale, static detail preview, and clean close-to-landing remount across devices.

lastAchievements: Increased SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE to 0.34 and raised mobile solar scale multipliers so the solar row appears larger on small touch viewports.
Changed the detail panel preview to a static GLB-like orb and removed the spinning CSS animation.
Changed scanner close recovery to remount a clean landing shell after AR teardown so stale A-Frame/AR.js layout side effects do not leave duplicate landing panels.
Hardened landing viewport recovery on close with repeated reset timers.
Added device-based solar-system scale multiplier for mobile by passing a computed multiplier into createArSceneMarkup.
Added session guards in bootScene so late AR boot steps do not mutate the UI after closing the scanner.
Closed the how-to modal and canceled delayed cleanup timers when stopping AR to avoid landing UI glitches.
Added Indonesian project docs (project brief, flow overview, API contract) and updated the architecture decision record and DESIGN scaling notes.
pendingIssues: Real-device verification is still required on Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and tablets after the latest scale multiplier changes.
If the solar row is still too small or too large, adjust getSolarScaleMultiplier or SOLAR_FALLBACK_SCALE in src/ar/scene.ts from the current 0.34 baseline.
If planet taps feel offset after scale changes, tune hitZonePosition and hitZoneRadius in src/data/planets.ts.
Verify the static planet preview orb sizing across planets; tweak previewScale values if any appear too large or too small.
If close still fails on a specific browser, capture DevTools source dimensions, body/html class list, and app DOM after close to decide whether a full page reload fallback is needed.
nextBestActions: Deploy and test open AR -> scan Hiro -> tap planet -> confirm static preview orb -> close detail -> close scanner -> reopen AR on iOS and Android.
Verify the landing page and modal remain centered and tappable after closing AR and after reopening AR.
Verify the solar row feels large enough on iPhone-sized viewports without clipping.
validationState: npm run build after larger solar scale, close remount, and static detail preview patch: PASS
docs/design-intent.json and active-memory JSON parse after documentation sync: PASS
npm run validate after latest UI changes: NOT RUN

designContinuity.currentConceptualAnchor: Handheld optical instrument panel (camera-first scanner HUD).
